content: High-quality CPR elements: compression rate 100-120/min, depth 5-6 cm (2-2.4 inches) in adults, full chest recoil between compressions, minimize interruptions (<10 seconds), avoid excessive ventilation. Compression-to-ventilation ratio 30:2 for single rescuer; continuous compressions with asynchronous ventilations once advanced airway placed. Hands-only CPR (compressions only) recommended for untrained bystanders—equivalent outcomes for adult sudden collapse. Mechanism: direct cardiac compression and thoracic pump generate blood flow (coronary perfusion pressure critical for ROSC). Feedback devices improve CPR quality. Switch compressors every 2 minutes to prevent fatigue. Defibrillation: biphasic shocks 120-200J (follow device-specific recommendations); resume CPR immediately after shock without rhythm check. Epinephrine 1mg IV/IO every 3-5 minutes improves ROSC and survival to discharge but not neurologically intact survival; give as soon as possible for non-shockable rhythms, after second shock for shockable rhythms. Amiodarone 300mg then 150mg for refractory VF/pVT.
